resources.string: UnScrolled detects which app is in the foreground and tracks scroll events to display a cooldown overlay and count your usage. No screen content is read.
Take back control of your attention
Notifications are the #1 trigger for compulsive scrolling. Disable them for social media apps — you won\'t miss anything important.
Before opening an app, ask yourself: "Do I need this for something specific?" If you can\'t answer in 2 seconds, the answer is no.
Keep your phone out of reach
Leave your phone in another room when working or spending time with family. Physical distance reduces temptation.
Wait at least 30 minutes after waking up before checking your phone. Start your day on your own terms, not by scrolling.
When you feel the urge to scroll, do something else: read a page of a book, do 10 push-ups, drink a glass of water. Your brain needs a replacement.
Apps are engineered to keep you hooked. Infinite scroll, notifications, likes — all calculated. Being aware of it is the first step.
Enable grayscale mode on your phone. Bright colours stimulate the brain and make scrolling more attractive.
Charge your phone away from bed
Don\'t charge your phone on your nightstand. Use a real alarm clock. Scrolling before sleep degrades sleep quality.
Don\'t try to quit everything at once. Cut 15 minutes per week. Slow progress is more sustainable than radical change.
Enable the Work profile in UnScrolled during working hours. You can configure a different set of blocked apps for work and home.

An independent app created to help fight scroll addiction — TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and more. The tips shared here come from personal experience. I\'m not a medical professional; adapt what works for you and seek help if needed.
All your data (scrolls, screen time, blocked apps) stays on your device. Nothing is sent or collected externally. Ever.
The app uses the Accessibility Service to detect when a blocked app is opened and to count scrolls. This permission is required for blocking and tracking to work — without it the app cannot function.
This app is actively developed. If you hit a bug or have a suggestion, your feedback genuinely helps improve it. Android only.
Create several profiles (e.g. Home, Work) each with their own list of blocked apps and settings. Switch manually or let the scheduler do it automatically.
When you try to open a blocked app, a countdown appears. This friction is intentional — it gives you a moment to ask yourself if you really need it.
Automatically activate a profile on specific days and time ranges — perfect for work hours or late-night focus sessions.
Track your daily scrolls, screen time on blocked apps, and how many times you tried to open them. Use the 7-day view to spot trends.
Set a maximum number of scrolls per day. When reached, blocked apps become fully inaccessible until midnight — no timer, no challenge.
Practical ways to reduce screen time
